title: Driven dynamics of an attractive Bose polaron
abstract: We study the out-of-equilibrium dynamics of an impurity driven by a constant external force through a system of homogeneous weakly-interacting bosons in one spatial dimension. The impurity-boson interaction is assumed to be attractive. We show that the impurity exhibits drifted Bloch oscillations in a wide range of forces in the absence of a lattice. We characterize the dynamical response of the host bosons and explain the mechanism underlying the Bloch oscillations. We analyse the behavior of the drift velocity, the Bloch amplitude and the time period of oscillations in a wide range of forces and other system parameters. In contrast to the case of repulsive impurity-boson interaction, the drift velocity exhibits a sub-linear dependence on a weak applied force, $V_d\sim {F}^α$ with a positive exponent $α$ smaller than unity. The drift velocity monotonically increases with force, though the scaling behavior varies considerably across different regimes of $F$. Moreover, the amplitude of the velocity oscillations displays rich behavior: it first undergoes a decay with force, reaches a minimum, and then presents a revival, increasing with force.
summary: The paper analyzes the driven dynamics of an impurity polaron coupled to a 1D Bose gas with attractive interactions. It demonstrates that the system exhibits drifted Bloch oscillations and derives unique scaling laws for the drift velocity ($V_d \sim F^\alpha$ with $\alpha < 1$). The research highlights how interaction strength and force fundamentally alter momentum transfer mechanisms in quantum gases.
main_problem: The study investigates the out-of-equilibrium dynamics of an impurity (polaron) driven by a constant external force through a 1D system of weakly-interacting bosons.
main_result: For attractive interactions, the drift velocity exhibits a sub-linear dependence on the applied force ($V_d \sim F^\alpha$ with $\alpha < 1$), and the Bloch oscillation amplitude shows rich non-monotonic behavior (decay $ightarrow$ minimum $ightarrow$ revival).
method: The analysis combines theoretical derivations of stationary solutions in the weak coupling limit ($\gamma \ll 1$) with numerical simulations solving the time-dependent Gross-Pitaevskii equation.
model_or_system: The model is a Bose polaron system in one dimension, described by an effective Hamiltonian involving the impurity momentum and the bosonic field. The interaction between the impurity and bosons is assumed to be attractive ($G<0$).
key_observables: Drift velocity ($V_d$), Bloch oscillation amplitude ($V_B$), oscillation period ($T$), and the total phase variation across the system.
important_parameters: The sign of the impurity-boson interaction ($G>0$ vs $G<0$), the strength of the external force ($F$), and the mass ratio ($M/m$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on the weak coupling limit ($\gamma \ll 1$) and compares results derived from stationary state solutions against full time-evolution dynamics.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate energy vs. momentum showing forbidden intervals, time evolution of velocity/density profiles demonstrating shockwave emission, and plots quantifying $V_d$, $V_B$, and $T$ dependence on the driving force $	ilde{F}$.
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes the dynamics by first establishing stationary solutions at zero force, then numerically simulating time evolution under a small driving force to observe drifted Bloch oscillations, and finally characterizing the scaling laws ($V_d \propto F^\alpha$) across different force regimes.
why_interesting: This work provides detailed insights into non-equilibrium transport phenomena in quantum many-body systems, particularly how attractive interactions modify fundamental dynamics like Bloch oscillations compared to repulsive cases.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
